key_insight: A cheap Gaussian-process surrogate can gate generative crystal proposals, preserving or improving Bayesian materials search efficiency while sharply reducing expensive oracle evaluations.
engineering_relevance: For closed-loop materials design, the workflow offers a practical way to cut evaluation cost without sacrificing discovery quality, making generative optimization more scalable for real materials screening.
method: Run an RL-steered generative loop with pretrained diffusion priors (MatterGen, CrystalFlow, ADiT).
Insert a Gaussian-process acquisition gate to rank generated structures before expensive property-oracle calls.
Use pretrained ORB embeddings as the strongest surrogate feature representation across multiple property types.
evidence_signal: Across two targets and three generators, the gated approach matched or exceeded ungated fine-tuning under a fixed oracle budget, came within about 9% of exhaustive oracle spending at roughly one-fifth the calls, and achieved Spearman ρ=0.94 for bulk-modulus ranking against DFT checks.
watch_item: Performance depends on surrogate ranking quality and pretrained embeddings, so the approach may weaken if the embedding-property pairing shifts or the surrogate is poorly calibrated on a new domain.
paper_title: Surrogate-Gated Generation and Foundation-Model Embeddings for Bayesian Materials Design
